title: settings.crossform_references is auto-populated when the form has cross-form calculations
description: OC4 documentation: 'Crossform References optimize loading forms by specifying the events or forms that the system needs to reference for cross-checks. Instead of the system loading all of a participant's information, it only loads the information necessary for the cross-check.' DEFAULT: whenever a form contains any calculate row using bind__oc_external=clinicaldata (cross-form reference), the skill auto-populates settings.crossform_references with the list of (event_oid, form_oid) pairs the calculations reference. The dependency graph is already in cross_form_dependencies in the spec JSON — this convention just propagates it. FORMAT: comma-separated list of EventOID/FormOID pairs in the crossform_references cell of the settings sheet. Override: manual entry in customer library; carry forward.
rationale: Without populated crossform_references, OC loads ALL of a participant's data every time a form with cross-form calculations renders, which is dramatically slower than loading only the referenced forms. The dependency information is already known at build time (the spec's cross_form_dependencies block captures it), so propagating to the form's settings sheet is a free performance optimization. Manual entry exists for cases where the customer wants to scope cross-form loading more broadly than the auto-detection captures.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} has cross-form calculations (bind__oc_external=clinicaldata) — settings.crossform_references must be populated with comma-separated EventOID/FormOID pairs from cross_form_dependencies. The dependency graph is already in the spec JSON; this rule just propagates it to the settings sheet.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §15 during Phase B.1d. Structured: applies_when filters on form.has_cross_form_calculations (boolean attribute on form derived from survey scan), effect uses require (raises flag if path is empty). Complements OC-4 (expressions.cross_form_xpath_patterns) — OC-4 validates the cross-form expression syntax; §15 propagates the resulting dependency metadata to the settings sheet for performance. No conflict, both apply.
